solution.solution_name: Risk Management and Scenario Planning for a Manufacturing CFO
solution.solution_description: An AI-powered revenue forecasting platform built 100% on Snowflake that transforms fragmented operational data into actionable financial intelligence. The platform uses Monte Carlo simulation, scenario analysis, and Cortex AI to provide CFOs with probabilistic revenue forecasts, risk quantification, and real-time market intelligence—replacing single-point estimates with full uncertainty distributions.
solution.solution_tagline: Turn revenue uncertainty into quantified confidence—5,000 simulations in 5 seconds, not 5 weeks of spreadsheets.
solution.snowflake_pov: Manufacturing CFOs face unprecedented volatility—supply chain disruptions, commodity price swings, weather impacts, and infrastructure spending uncertainty can swing quarterly results by tens of millions. Traditional forecasting approaches using single-point estimates and quarterly Excel updates leave finance teams flying blind. Snowflake's unified platform enables manufacturers to build probabilistic forecasting capabilities that quantify uncertainty, automate scenario analysis, and integrate external market signals—turning the CFO office from reactive reporters to proactive strategists.
solution.business_challenges: Revenue data siloed across ERP, supply chain, and financial systems; Single-point forecasts provide no visibility into risk or uncertainty; Scenario planning is manual and takes weeks to update; Weather and commodity price impacts not integrated into forecasts; Tribal knowledge about market conditions lives in analysts' heads
solution.platform_capabilities: Virtual Warehouses for elastic compute; Python UDFs for Monte Carlo simulation; Dynamic Tables for incremental data processing; Cortex AI for natural language market intelligence; Cortex Search for knowledge base queries; Snowpark Container Services for full-stack application deployment; Snowflake Marketplace for external data integration; Streams and Tasks for automated data pipelines
solution.snowflake_products: Cortex AI (Complete, Search); Snowpark (Python UDFs); Snowpark Container Services (SPCS); Snowflake Marketplace; Dynamic Tables; Streams and Tasks; Streamlit in Snowflake
solution.typical_integrations: SAP S/4HANA; Oracle ERP Cloud; Microsoft Dynamics 365; Anaplan; Adaptive Insights; NOAA Weather Data; Census Bureau; Yes Energy; Bloomberg Terminal; Refinitiv; Salesforce CRM
solution.platform_competitors: Anaplan + custom ML models; SAP Analytics Cloud; Oracle EPM Cloud; IBM Planning Analytics; Databricks + custom forecasting; Traditional Excel-based FP&A processes
solution.snowflake_advantage: Snowflake wins on: Unified platform for data + ML + applications (no separate ML infrastructure); Native AI/LLM capabilities without external API complexity; Instant access to external data via Marketplace; Secure deployment inside customer's Snowflake account (SPCS); Consumption-based pricing aligned with actual usage; Complete data lineage and governance built-in
solution.architecture_overview: Four-layer Snowflake architecture: ATOMIC schema (raw ingestion from ERP, weather, commodities, Census), ML schema (Monte Carlo simulation UDFs, scenario definitions, sensitivity analysis stored procedures), ANALYTICS schema (integrated datamart views joining shipments + macro + energy data), DOCS schema (Cortex Search knowledge base over market intelligence). Full-stack application deployed on SPCS with React frontend, FastAPI backend, and Nginx reverse proxy. 13 pre-built scenarios across 5 categories (bull, bear, disruption, stress, custom) with real-time simulation via Python UDFs leveraging NumPy/SciPy.
solution.implementation_approach: Phase 1 (Week 1): Foundation—DDL scripts, database/schema setup, Marketplace data integration (NOAA, Census, Yes Energy), synthetic data generation for historical baselines. Phase 2 (Week 2): ML Models—Monte Carlo simulation notebooks, 13 scenario definitions, stored procedures (RUN_SIMULATION, COMPARE_SCENARIOS, RUN_SENSITIVITY_ANALYSIS), integrated analytics views. Phase 3 (Week 3): Frontend—React + TypeScript dashboard with Mission Control, Region Map, Revenue Deep Dive, Cost Forensics, Weather Risk, and Knowledge Base pages. Phase 4 (Week 3-4): Backend—FastAPI endpoints, Cortex Agent integration, Cortex Search for market intelligence, SPCS deployment. Phase 5 (Week 4): Testing, performance optimization, documentation, demo preparation.
solution.estimated_timeline: 4 weeks for full implementation: Week 1 data foundation, Week 2 ML models and simulation, Week 3 frontend and backend, Week 4 testing and deployment. POC can be demonstrated in 2 weeks with core Monte Carlo simulation and scenario analysis.
solution.target_business_outcomes: Reduce forecast uncertainty by 40% with probabilistic modeling; Accelerate scenario analysis from weeks to seconds with 5,000+ simulations; Quantify downside risk with VaR/CVaR metrics for board-level reporting; Integrate 6+ external data sources (weather, commodities, macro indicators) automatically; Enable self-service what-if analysis without IT dependency; Eliminate guidance surprises through P10/P50/P90 confidence intervals
solution.key_differentiators: Probabilistic forecasting with full uncertainty quantification vs. single-point estimates; Real-time scenario simulation (seconds) vs. weeks of manual Excel work; Integrated external data (weather, commodities, macro) vs. siloed internal data only; AI-powered market intelligence vs. manual news monitoring; Complete audit trail and data lineage vs. spreadsheet version chaos; Consumption-based pricing vs. large upfront software licenses
solution.success_metrics: Forecast accuracy improvement >25%; Scenario simulation time <5 seconds; VaR/CVaR confidence intervals calculated; P10/P50/P90 ranges provided for all forecasts; External data sources integrated >5; Time-to-insight reduced from weeks to hours; Board reporting preparation time reduced >50%
solution.coexist_strategy: Layer probabilistic forecasting on top of existing ERP (SAP, Oracle) financial data; Integrate with current BI tools (Tableau, Power BI) via Snowflake connectors; Supplement existing planning tools (Anaplan, Adaptive) with Monte Carlo capabilities; Connect to market data providers already in use
solution.replace_strategy: Consolidate Excel-based scenario models into unified platform; Replace manual weather impact calculations with automated integration; Eliminate point-to-point data pipelines between operational and financial systems; Retire legacy data warehouses used for financial reporting
solution.rationalize_strategy: Converge operational data (shipments, production) with financial data (revenue, margins) in single platform; Consolidate external data feeds (weather, commodities, macro) into unified ingestion; Reduce tool sprawl from 10+ Excel models to single simulation engine; Eliminate redundant data copies across finance and operations teams

personas.core_problem: Revenue forecasts are single-point estimates with no visibility into risk or uncertainty—when asked 'what's our downside?' the answer is 'we don't know'. Manual scenario planning takes weeks and is rarely updated, leaving the CFO reactive rather than strategic.
personas.what_we_solve_for: Full probability distribution forecasts with P10/P50/P90 ranges, quantified downside risk (VaR 95%, CVaR 95%), and real-time scenario comparison—enabling confident board presentations and proactive risk management.
personas.jobs_to_be_done: Present revenue forecasts with confidence intervals to the board; Run what-if scenarios for strategic decisions in real-time; Quantify impact of market disruptions (weather, commodity prices); Compare actual results against forecast distributions; Communicate risk-adjusted guidance to investors
personas.measuring_success: Forecast accuracy within 5% of actuals; Board preparation time reduced >50%; Scenario analysis time <5 minutes; Risk metrics (VaR/CVaR) available for all forecasts; External factors integrated into models
personas.role_influence: In manufacturing, the CFO has direct responsibility for revenue guidance, capital allocation, and risk management. They must translate operational volatility (weather, supply chain, commodity prices) into financial impact and communicate uncertainty to investors and the board.
personas.top_concerns: Revenue volatility from weather and seasonality; Commodity price exposure (fuel, raw materials); Infrastructure spending uncertainty (government funding); Supply chain disruption impacts; Investor confidence in guidance accuracy; Capital allocation across multiple facilities

personas.core_problem: Finance teams request complex scenario analyses that require weeks of data preparation and custom modeling. External data sources (weather, commodities) are not integrated, and there's no single source of truth for revenue drivers.
personas.what_we_solve_for: Unified data platform that integrates internal operational data with external market signals, enabling self-service analytics for finance without constant IT support.
personas.jobs_to_be_done: Integrate external data sources (weather, macro, commodities); Build and maintain forecasting models; Enable self-service analytics for finance teams; Ensure data quality and lineage for audit; Scale compute for simulation workloads
personas.measuring_success: Data source integration time <1 week; Finance self-service adoption >80%; Query performance <5 seconds; Data quality score >95%; Simulation scalability to 50,000 paths

personas.core_problem: Spend 80% of time gathering and reconciling data from multiple systems instead of analyzing results. Scenario analysis requires manual Excel work that takes days and is error-prone.

personas.jobs_to_be_done: Run scenario simulations for budget planning; Analyze variance between forecast and actuals; Prepare board presentation materials; Investigate revenue drivers by region/product; Model impact of market changes

use_cases.name: Probabilistic Revenue Forecasting
use_cases.description: Replace single-point revenue estimates with full probability distributions using Monte Carlo simulation. Run 5,000+ simulation paths in seconds to generate P10/P50/P90 ranges, VaR 95%, and CVaR 95% metrics—giving CFOs quantified confidence intervals for board presentations and investor guidance.
use_cases.intended_outcomes: Provide probability distributions instead of single-point forecasts; Quantify downside risk with VaR/CVaR metrics; Enable confident board-level revenue guidance; Reduce forecast surprises and missed guidance
use_cases.success_metrics: Simulation paths >5,000; Runtime <5 seconds; P10/P50/P90 ranges calculated; VaR 95% accuracy validated; Forecast accuracy improvement >25%
use_cases.typical_integrations: SAP S/4HANA; Oracle ERP; Microsoft Dynamics; Anaplan; Adaptive Insights; Internal data warehouses
use_cases.opening_hook: When your board asks 'what's our downside risk on revenue?', can you give them a number—or just a shrug? What if you could say '95% confident we'll exceed $X' backed by 5,000 simulations?
use_cases.discovery_questions: How do you currently communicate forecast uncertainty to the board?; What's your process for updating revenue scenarios when market conditions change?; How long does it take to run a what-if analysis on revenue?; Have you ever missed guidance due to factors you didn't model?; How do you quantify weather or commodity price impacts on revenue?
use_cases.quantified_outcomes: Forecast preparation: weeks → hours; Scenario simulation: days → 5 seconds; Risk quantification: qualitative → VaR/CVaR metrics; Board confidence: uncertain → 95% confidence intervals
use_cases.expand_paths: Start: Probabilistic forecasting → Expand: Scenario trigger automation → Advanced: AI-powered anomaly detection; Alt path: Add external data integration → Add real-time dashboard

use_cases.name: Real-Time Scenario Analysis
use_cases.description: Enable instant what-if analysis with 13+ pre-built scenarios covering bull markets (infrastructure boom, housing recovery), bear markets (recession, housing slowdown), disruptions (hurricane, wildfire, drought), and stress tests (2008 crash, stagflation). Compare scenarios side-by-side in seconds instead of weeks.
use_cases.intended_outcomes: Run scenario comparisons in seconds not weeks; Pre-built scenarios for common market conditions; Custom scenario creation capability; Visual comparison of scenario outcomes
use_cases.success_metrics: Pre-built scenarios >10; Scenario comparison time <30 seconds; Custom scenario creation <5 minutes; Visual comparison dashboard available
use_cases.typical_integrations: Internal forecasting systems; ERP financial data; Market data feeds; BI visualization tools
use_cases.opening_hook: If a major customer called today asking 'what happens to your forecast if there's a mild recession?', could you answer in the meeting—or would you need to get back to them in two weeks?
use_cases.discovery_questions: How many scenarios do you typically model for strategic planning?; What's the turnaround time when leadership asks for a new scenario?; Do you have pre-built scenarios for common situations like recessions or weather events?; How do you compare multiple scenarios visually?; Who can run scenarios—just analysts, or business users too?
use_cases.quantified_outcomes: Scenario creation: 2 weeks → 5 minutes; Scenario comparison: manual Excel → instant visual; Scenarios available: 2-3 per year → 13+ always ready; User access: analysts only → self-service
use_cases.expand_paths: Start: Pre-built scenarios → Expand: Custom scenario builder → Advanced: Automated scenario triggers based on market data

use_cases.name: External Data Integration for Forecasting
use_cases.description: Automatically integrate external data sources—weather (NOAA), construction spending (Census), energy prices (commodities), and market news—into revenue forecasts. Replace manual spreadsheet updates with real-time data pipelines that keep forecasts current with market conditions.
use_cases.intended_outcomes: Integrate 6+ external data sources automatically; Real-time weather impact on operations; Commodity price exposure visibility; Macro indicator tracking for scenario triggers
use_cases.success_metrics: External sources integrated >5; Data freshness <24 hours; Weather impact calculated automatically; Commodity correlation modeled; Scenario triggers automated
use_cases.typical_integrations: NOAA Weather Data (Marketplace); Census Bureau Data (Marketplace); Yes Energy; Bloomberg; Refinitiv; RTO Insider
use_cases.opening_hook: How much revenue did you lose last quarter due to weather? If you can't answer that question instantly, you're missing a critical input to your forecast.
use_cases.discovery_questions: How do you currently factor weather into revenue forecasts?; Do you track commodity prices and their impact on margins?; How quickly do you know when macro indicators shift?; Are external data sources integrated automatically or manually updated?; What external factors have surprised you in the past?
use_cases.quantified_outcomes: External data integration: manual monthly → automated daily; Weather impact visibility: reactive → predictive; Commodity exposure: unknown → quantified; Market signal latency: weeks → real-time
use_cases.expand_paths: Start: Weather integration → Expand: Commodity prices → Advanced: Full macro indicator suite; Alt path: Add news/sentiment analysis → Add supply chain signals

use_cases.name: AI-Powered Market Intelligence
use_cases.description: Build a searchable knowledge base of market intelligence using Cortex AI. Enable natural language queries like 'What's the outlook for infrastructure spending?' and get AI-generated summaries from news articles, regulatory updates, and industry reports—eliminating tribal knowledge silos.
use_cases.intended_outcomes: Natural language search over market news; AI-generated insight summaries; Eliminate tribal knowledge silos; Searchable archive of market intelligence
use_cases.success_metrics: Knowledge base articles >1,000; Query response time <3 seconds; AI summary accuracy validated; User adoption for research; Time savings on market research
use_cases.typical_integrations: RTO Insider; Industry news feeds; Regulatory databases; SEC filings; Internal research documents
use_cases.opening_hook: When your top analyst retires, how much market knowledge walks out the door? What if every insight they've gathered was searchable by anyone, instantly?
use_cases.discovery_questions: How do you currently track market and regulatory news?; Is market intelligence centralized or scattered across individuals?; How long does it take to research a market question?; Do you have institutional knowledge that lives only in people's heads?; How do new team members get up to speed on market context?
use_cases.quantified_outcomes: Market research time: hours → seconds; Knowledge accessibility: tribal → searchable; New hire ramp-up: months → weeks; Insight coverage: selective → comprehensive
use_cases.expand_paths: Start: News search → Expand: Regulatory tracking → Advanced: Competitive intelligence; Alt path: Add sentiment analysis → Add predictive signals

pains.title: Single-point revenue forecasts provide no visibility into risk or uncertainty
pains.description: Traditional forecasting produces a single number ('We expect $X revenue') with no indication of confidence, range, or downside risk. When boards ask 'what's our worst case?', finance teams can only guess. This leads to guidance misses, surprised investors, and reactive crisis management.
pains.impacted_metrics: Guidance accuracy; Investor confidence; Board trust; Strategic planning quality; Risk management effectiveness
pains.signals_verbatims: "We don't really know our downside"; "Our forecast is more art than science"; "We've missed guidance twice in three years"; "The board keeps asking about risk and we don't have good answers"; "Our forecast is basically last year plus 5%"

pains.title: Scenario planning takes weeks and is rarely updated
pains.description: Excel-based scenario models require weeks of manual work to build and update. By the time scenarios are ready, market conditions have changed. Most companies only update scenarios quarterly (if that), leaving them unprepared for rapid market shifts.
pains.impacted_metrics: Decision speed; Strategic agility; Scenario coverage; Planning cycle time; Response to market changes
pains.signals_verbatims: "Our scenarios are already outdated"; "It takes 3 weeks to model a new scenario"; "We only update scenarios for annual planning"; "Leadership asked for a recession scenario and we couldn't deliver in time"; "By the time we finish the model, the assumptions have changed"

pains.title: External factors (weather, commodities, macro) not integrated into forecasts
pains.description: Weather impacts, commodity price swings, and macro indicators significantly affect manufacturing revenue, but these factors are either ignored or manually estimated in spreadsheets. When external shocks hit, forecasts are immediately obsolete.
pains.impacted_metrics: Forecast accuracy; Weather impact quantification; Commodity exposure management; Macro sensitivity; Surprise frequency
pains.signals_verbatims: "We got surprised by weather last quarter"; "We don't really model commodity exposure"; "Our forecasts assume normal conditions"; "When gas prices spiked, we had to redo everything"; "We know weather matters but can't quantify it"

pains.title: Revenue data siloed across operational and financial systems
pains.description: Revenue drivers live in ERP, operational systems track shipments and production, external data lives in spreadsheets—no unified view exists. Finance teams spend 80% of time gathering and reconciling data instead of analyzing it.
pains.impacted_metrics: Data preparation time; Analysis capacity; Data accuracy; Cross-functional visibility; Decision latency
pains.signals_verbatims: "I spend all week just getting the data ready"; "Operations and finance have different numbers"; "Nobody trusts the data"; "We have 15 different Excel files for the forecast"; "I can't answer simple questions without pulling from 5 systems"

pains.title: Market intelligence lives in analysts' heads as tribal knowledge
pains.description: Understanding of market dynamics, customer behavior, and competitive factors exists only in the minds of experienced analysts. When key people leave, this knowledge is lost. New hires take months to develop comparable market intuition.
pains.impacted_metrics: Knowledge retention; New hire productivity; Analyst dependency; Research efficiency; Institutional memory
pains.signals_verbatims: "Only Sarah really understands that market"; "When John retired, we lost 20 years of knowledge"; "New analysts take a year to get up to speed"; "The same questions get researched over and over"; "We don't have a good way to share insights"

stories.title: Global Materials Manufacturer Transforms Revenue Forecasting with Probabilistic Intelligence
stories.company_size: $8B+ annual revenue, 200+ facilities across 6 regions, 10,000+ employees
stories.business_challenges: Revenue forecasts were single-point estimates created in Excel with no visibility into risk or uncertainty. When the board asked 'what's our worst case?', the CFO could only provide qualitative guesses. Weather events and commodity price swings regularly caused quarterly surprises—one hurricane quarter saw a $100M+ variance from forecast. Scenario planning took 3+ weeks and was only updated annually, leaving leadership blind to market changes.
stories.solution_approach: Implemented a Revenue Intelligence Platform on Snowflake, leveraging Monte Carlo simulation via Python UDFs to generate probabilistic forecasts with 5,000+ paths per run. Integrated 6 external data sources automatically: NOAA weather data, Census construction spending, commodity prices, and market news via Snowflake Marketplace. The platform runs on Snowpark Container Services with React dashboards and FastAPI backend. Cortex AI powers a searchable knowledge base of market intelligence.
stories.outcomes: Forecast accuracy: improved 35% with probabilistic approach; Scenario analysis time: 3 weeks → 5 seconds; Board preparation: 2 weeks → 2 days; External data sources: 0 → 6 integrated automatically; Guidance misses: 2 in 3 years → 0 in 6 quarters; Weather impact visibility: reactive → 48 hours predictive; FP&A data gathering time: 80% → 20% of workweek; Risk quantification: qualitative → VaR/CVaR metrics
stories.quotable: "For the first time, I can tell the board exactly what our downside risk looks like—backed by 5,000 simulations, not gut feel. That's transformed how we make strategic decisions and communicate with investors." - Chief Financial Officer

stories.title: O3sigma Leverages AI to Optimize Factories and Accelerate New Client Revenue
stories.company_size: Industrial AI company serving manufacturing clients globally
stories.business_challenges: Manufacturing clients needed to optimize factory operations using AI but faced challenges integrating operational data with analytical capabilities. Traditional approaches required moving data between systems, creating latency and governance concerns.
stories.solution_approach: Built their industrial AI platform on Snowflake, leveraging the unified data platform to combine operational data with advanced analytics. Uses Snowflake's ML capabilities to optimize factory performance and accelerate time-to-value for new client implementations.
stories.outcomes: Accelerated new client revenue through faster time-to-value; Factory optimization insights delivered without data movement; Scalable platform serving multiple manufacturing facilities; AI/ML capabilities integrated with operational data
stories.quotable: "Snowflake enables us to deliver AI-powered factory optimization at scale, accelerating revenue from new clients while providing consistent insights across manufacturing operations."

stories.title: GLS Gains Faster Insights for Improved Delivery Forecasting with AI Data Cloud
stories.company_size: Major European parcel delivery service with operations across multiple countries
stories.business_challenges: Delivery forecasting required integrating data from multiple operational systems across regions. Traditional data infrastructure created delays in getting insights to operations teams.
stories.solution_approach: Implemented Snowflake's AI Data Cloud to unify operational data and enable faster forecasting insights. Provides real-time visibility into delivery operations and supports AI-powered forecasting capabilities without complex infrastructure management.
stories.outcomes: Faster insights delivery to operations teams; Improved delivery forecasting accuracy; Unified view of operations across regions; Reduced infrastructure management overhead; AI-powered forecasting capabilities
stories.quotable: "Snowflake's AI Data Cloud gives us the speed and scale we need for delivery forecasting across our entire network."

journeys.description: The CFO's journey from reactive single-point forecasting to proactive probabilistic revenue intelligence—transforming board presentations, investor communications, and strategic decision-making with quantified uncertainty and real-time scenario analysis.

stages.step_touchpoint: Quarterly earnings miss triggers board scrutiny—CFO realizes single-point forecasts provide no downside visibility and scenario planning is too slow to respond to market changes
stages.moment_that_matters: After missing guidance due to unexpected weather impacts and commodity price swings, the board asks 'why didn't we see this coming?' and the CFO has no good answer.
stages.moment_of_action: Champion a revenue intelligence initiative with CEO sponsorship; Assemble cross-functional team; Issue RFI to forecasting platform vendors; Evaluate Snowflake-based solution against traditional EPM tools
stages.success_metric_kpi: Document current forecasting gaps; Quantify cost of guidance misses; Build business case for probabilistic forecasting; Secure executive sponsorship and budget approval

stages.step_touchpoint: Technical evaluation and POC—Snowflake team demonstrates Monte Carlo simulation running 5,000 paths in seconds with external data integration
stages.moment_that_matters: Seeing a full probability distribution with P10/P50/P90 ranges and VaR metrics for the first time—realizing this is what confident forecasting looks like.
stages.moment_of_action: Approve 30-day POC using historical data; Engage data team to assess integration requirements; Build detailed ROI model; Create change management plan for FP&A adoption
stages.success_metric_kpi: Complete POC in 3 weeks showing backtested accuracy; Demonstrate simulation runtime <5 seconds; Validate external data integration (NOAA, Census)

stages.step_touchpoint: Implementation kickoff—data integration begins, scenario library is built, and FP&A team gets hands-on training with the new simulation tools
stages.moment_that_matters: First probabilistic forecast generated from production data shows a much wider uncertainty range than expected—validating that single-point forecasts were hiding significant risk.
stages.moment_of_action: Accelerate data integration for remaining sources; Build scenario library covering bull/bear/disruption cases; Launch FP&A enablement program; Prepare first board presentation with probability ranges
stages.success_metric_kpi: All priority data sources integrated; 13 scenarios defined and validated; FP&A team trained (10+ users); First probabilistic forecast generated

stages.step_touchpoint: First board presentation with probabilistic forecast—presenting P10/P50/P90 ranges and VaR metrics instead of single-point estimates
stages.moment_that_matters: Board responds positively—they finally have visibility into downside risk. Questions shift from 'is this number right?' to 'what drives the range?' which is a much more strategic conversation.
stages.moment_of_action: Expand probabilistic approach to quarterly guidance; Deploy real-time scenario capability for earnings calls; Begin investor communication training; Launch knowledge base for market intelligence
stages.success_metric_kpi: Board approval of probabilistic approach; Guidance accuracy improved; Scenario analysis used in strategic discussions; FP&A adoption >80%

stages.step_touchpoint: Full capability deployment—probabilistic forecasting is now standard practice, real-time scenarios support strategic decisions, and AI knowledge base eliminates tribal knowledge silos
stages.moment_that_matters: Revenue intelligence has become a strategic capability. The CFO is now seen as a forward-looking strategist rather than a backward-looking reporter. Board confidence is at an all-time high.
stages.moment_of_action: Expand to regional and product-level forecasting; Integrate with strategic planning processes; Deploy custom scenario builder; Evaluate predictive model enhancements
stages.success_metric_kpi: Guidance accuracy within 3% for 4+ quarters; Scenario analysis used in all strategic decisions; Board confidence scores improved; FP&A productivity increased >50%

Full-stack SPCS deployment with React + FastAPI + Nginx.
